introduction.now: I'm an Italian software engineer currently working at {Appetize}.
introduction.passion: I love writing code both professionally and in my spare time. I’m at my best when I’m learning, experimenting with new technologies, and tackling complex problems.
introduction.education: I hold a MSc in Advanced Computer Science from the {uom} and a trilingual BSc from the {unibz}, with an exchange semester at the {cofc}.
introduction.work: Over the past eight years, I’ve worked across a range of companies and products, including {Booking}, {BJSS} and {Resourcify}, building and scaling web applications in distributed teams.
introduction.cv: I hold a MSc in Advanced Computer Science from the University of Manchester, and have spent the last 6 years working for several companies, including {Booking}, {BJSS} and {Resourcify}.
introduction.freeTime: Outside of tech, I’m a fiction writer ✍🏻, an avid bookworm 📚, an oboist and alto singer 🎶, and a travel photographer 📷.

skills.fullStack: While I have a solid foundation in both frontend and backend development, my focus and passion are firmly in full-stack engineering. I enjoy working across the stack, from designing APIs and data models to building intuitive, well-crafted user interfaces.
skills.languages: My experience spans multiple languages — including Java, Kotlin, Python and TypeScript — and frontend frameworks such as React and Angular. Recently, I’ve been working extensively in the Node.js ecosystem, using Next.js with an OpenAPI-based backend and MongoDB, as well as AstroJS to build fast, content-driven websites.
skills.latest: Alongside development, I’ve been deepening my interest in UI and UX, learning to design interfaces in Figma and to use Tailwind CSS effectively to build accessible, cohesive layouts.

reading: 'We Should All Be Feminists', by C.N. Adichie
'The War on Science', by Lawrence M. Krauss
